title: Crime is dropping fast in Syracuse. Police chief says it’s a snowball effect
summary: Syracuse crime has declined 27% year-to-date (through Aug. 11, 2025), with violent crime down 18% and property crime down 29%—reversing a three-year property crime increase trend. Police Chief Joe Cecile attributes gains to the Mayor's Office to Reduce Gun Violence (launched 2022), which deploys ~25 'credible messengers' for community violence intervention, combined with proactive policing strategies. Gun violence data shows 44 people injured/killed through Aug. 11 versus a five-year average of 88, indicating sustained momentum in a key public-safety metric.
